description:
Congressman Alcee L. Hastings (FL-20) is seeking highly motivated, detail oriented, and energetic applicants for a full or part-time unpaid internship in Washington, D.C. to begin immediately.

Qualified applicants have strong writing and communication skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks, and the willingness to learn. Primary responsibilities include answering phones, conducting legislative research and drafting memos, and assisting in managing the Congressman's official correspondence.

Congressman Hastings supports students requesting credit for their internship through their school or university. To apply please visit https://alceehastings.house.gov/forms/internships/. NO phone calls or drop-ins please.

This is an in-person internship. Applicants must be willing to wear a mask and adhere to strict social distancing and sanitation guidelines. Women and minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Conducting legislative research and drafting memos
Assisting in managing the Congressman's official correspondence
qualifications: Strong writing and communication skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks
Willingness to learn
how_to_apply: To apply please visit https://alceehastings.house.gov/forms/internships/. NO phone calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: Women and minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Kweisi Mfume (MD-07) is seeking a motivated, detail-oriented intern to work remotely in a fast-paced Congressional office.

Intern responsibilities include answering phones, researching legislation, attending virtual hearings and briefings, and answering constituent letters on various issues before the House. Interns will also assist in various administrative tasks as needed. As a result, the intern will learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.

The term of this paid internship will be from January to April 2021. Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, interns will solely work remotely for the duration of the internship and be provided the appropriate work equipment. The position is part-time (~20 hours a week).
responsibilities: Answering constituent letters on various issues before the House
Assist in various administrative tasks as needed
qualifications: Motivated and detail-oriented
Availability from January to April 2021
Able to work part-time (approximately 20 hours a week)
District and Maryland ties encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, short writing sample, and dates of availability to Md07employment@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2021 Congressional Internship" in the subject line. The deadline to submit applications is January 20th at 5:00 p.m. ET. No phone calls, follow-ups or drop-ins, please.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description:
The Office of Congressman Kweisi Mfume (MD-07) is seeking a Digital Press Intern to assist with a fast-paced communications operation for Spring 2021. This is a paid, part-time position (~20 hours a week). Qualified candidates will have strong writing, video editing, social media, graphic design, and photography skills, and be able to work on tight deadlines.

Digital Press Intern responsibilities include, but are not limited to, content creation, graphic design, manual photography, videography, website maintenance, analytics, researching legislation, answering phones, and performing other press and administrative tasks as necessary. Successful candidates will possess familiarity with programs such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, Canva, as well as compiling and interpreting social media analytics.

Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, interns will solely work remotely for the duration of the internship and be provided the appropriate work equipment. The selected candidate will report to the Communications Director.
responsibilities: Performing other press and administrative tasks as necessary
qualifications: Strong writing, video editing, social media, graphic design, and photography skills
Ability to work on tight deadlines
Familiarity with programs such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, Canva
Ability to compile and interpret social media analytics
District and Maryland ties are encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, portfolio of created content, and dates of availability to Md07employment@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2021 Digital Press Internship" in the subject line. No phone calls, follow-ups or drop-ins, please. The deadline to submit applications is January 20th at 5:00 p.m. ET.
salary_info: Paid, part-time position (~20 hours a week)
equal_opportunity: Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

office: House Foreign Affairs Committee Majority Staff
description:
The House Foreign Affairs Committee Majority Staff is currently looking for interns for the Spring 2021 semester.

Interns will be responsible for providing staff members with policy and administrative support. Duties may include answering phones, compiling press clips, attending staff briefings, performing legislative and policy research, drafting memoranda and other written materials, and a variety of other tasks.

Candidates should be team-oriented, resourceful, upbeat people who have skills beyond research and require little supervision. This internship is open to undergraduates (juniors and seniors), recent graduates, and Master's and law students. Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Provide staff members with policy and administrative support
Draft memoranda and other written materials
qualifications: Team-oriented, resourceful, and upbeat
Possess skills beyond research
Able to work with little supervision
Undergraduate juniors or seniors, recent graduates, or Master's and law students
how_to_apply: Submit a one-page resume and cover letter in PDF format to hcfa.democrat@mail.house.gov and include "HFAC Internship Application - Spring 2021" in the subject line of the email.
equal_opportunity: Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
Congressman John Garamendi (CA-03) is seeking a highly motivated intern to join his team.

Candidates should be collegial, professional, quick learners, and phenomenal team players. Strong oral and written communication skills and close attention to detail are imperative. Duties include assisting staff with legislative, administrative, and press related projects, writing memos and talking points, attending virtual hearings and meetings, answering constituent phone calls, assisting with scheduling, sorting and distributing mail, and managing tour and flag requests.

Interns are considered an integral part of the team and are given the opportunity to learn and grow through working with us both to achieve the goals of the office and to pursue and develop their areas of interest. First-hand experience in Congressman Garamendi's congressional office provides interns with an unparalleled knowledge and understanding of the development of public policy.

Full-time candidates with California ties and previous internship/work experience are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Assisting staff with legislative, administrative, and press related projects
qualifications: Collegial, professional, quick learners, and phenomenal team players
Strong oral and written communication skills
Close attention to detail
California ties preferred
Previous internship/work experience preferred
how_to_apply: Please e-mail a resume, cover letter, writing sample and dates of availability to Garamendi.Internships@mail.house.gov

description: The office of Congressman Jeff Van Drew (NJ-02) is currently accepting applications for a part-time social media intern for the spring internship session. The start date could be as early as Jan 4 (flexible depending on availability) with a flexible end date. Internships are paid, and transit benefits will be provided. The internship will offer students and young professionals invaluable experience with our nation's legislative process. Interns contribute to the daily operations of the office and are expected to act in a reliable, professional manner at all times.
responsibilities: Assist staff with various other tasks
qualifications: Current student or young professional
Ability to act in a reliable and professional manner
Availability for spring internship session
how_to_apply: Email a resume and cover letter to JeffVanDrewNJ@gmail.com. Indicate in your cover letter that you are applying for a spring internship and format the files as [Full Name_Resume], [Full Name_Cover Letter].
